name: CAIR FOUNDATION INC
mission: Communications Department works in conjunction with local and national media to ensure an accurate portrayal of Islam and Muslims is presented to the American public.
theoryOfChange: CAIR Foundation has published a '2024 General Election CAIR's Strategic Election Plan' which outlines specific goals and actions to achieve them, such as increasing Muslim voter registration, educating on policy, advocating for peace in Palestine, and fighting Islamophobia. A similar document, 'CAIR'S STRATEGIC ELECTION PLAN,' details electoral programs and goals for the 2020 and 2022 elections. Furthermore, Fahrenheit Advisors facilitated CAIR's strategic planning process and created a scorecard of new performance metrics, indicating a documented approach to measuring change. Charity Navigator also highlights CAIR's mission and goals related to advocacy, civil rights, and community empowerment, suggesting an underlying strategic framework for impact and measurement.
scoreSummary: CAIR FOUNDATION INC shows good alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance.

evaluationSources: Congressional Research Service (2010 report) cited CAIR's cooperation with authorities

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.cair.com/zakat (Source: https://www.cair.com/zakat)

dimension_explanations.alignment: With a medium donor fit and a focus on the urgent cause of advocacy, the organization is well-aligned for Muslim donors seeking to support civil rights [2]. It is officially zakat-eligible, distributing funds according to established asnaf categories to support those facing discrimination or hardship [2][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through civil rights defense and research programs [4]. While it maintains robust financial health with 11.4 months of working capital, the impact score of 27/50 reflects the inherent difficulty in quantifying the direct outcomes of broad advocacy and media relations work [1][3].

confidence.notes: Strong alignment between third-party ratings and internal financial reporting.

alignment.explanation: CAIR is a strong fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="21">zakat-eligible giving</cite> specifically for the <cite id="22">fisabilillah (in the path of Allah)</cite> category.
alignment.improvement: Clarify <cite id="23">zakat allocation policies</cite> and specific eligible use categories to provide greater transparency for religious donors.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust CAIR due to its <cite id="11">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and <cite id="14">88% independent board</cite> that ensures objective oversight.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization could <cite id="19">publish more granular beneficiary data</cite> to allow for external cost-effectiveness validation.

impact.improvement: Publish <cite id="19">consistent beneficiary counts</cite> so that cost-effectiveness can be assessed across its various advocacy and legal programs.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize American Muslim civil rights and want to support a long-standing institution with high transparency and zakat eligibility.
donor_motivations: Want to protect the civil liberties of Muslims in the U.S.
Value high third-party accountability ratings (Charity Navigator 98/100)
Prefer supporting established organizations with a 30-year track record
Seek to fulfill zakat through advocacy (fisabilillah)
giving_considerations: Gifts support systemic change rather than immediate individual relief
High executive pay is a factor for some donors to consider
Impact is measured through policy and legal wins rather than headcounts
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking direct humanitarian aid (e.g., food/medicine) or those who prefer organizations with low executive compensation ratios.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcomes and a strong theory of change, but lacks independent cost-effectiveness evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: CAIR uses legal action, media pressure, and legislative advocacy to shift public perception and protect civil rights.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts translate into tangible legal protections for the community.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a $9.4M funding gap and rising Islamophobia, CAIR has significant capacity to expand its legal and media departments.

strengths.detail: The organization holds a <cite id="7">98.33/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite> and has achieved the <cite id="11">Candid Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>.
CAIR operates with lean development costs, spending only <cite id="12">$0.19 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
The charity maintains <cite id="9">11.9 months of operating reserves</cite>, ensuring stability during economic or political shifts.

strengths_deep_dive: CAIR's operational strength lies in its dual-track approach of legal defense and proactive media engagement. The organization's <cite id="13">Accountability & Finance beacon score of 98</cite> reflects a strong independent board, with <cite id="14">88% independent members</cite>. Furthermore, its ability to scale is evidenced by its <cite id="5">$7.9 million revenue</cite> base, which is largely driven by <cite id="15">$7.55 million in direct public contributions</cite>, signaling deep community trust.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1994</cite>, the CAIR Foundation Inc. (Council on American-Islamic Relations) has established itself as the leading <cite id="2">civil rights and advocacy organization</cite> for the American Muslim community. Operating with a mission to <cite id="3">ensure an accurate portrayal of Islam</cite> and protect civil liberties, the organization manages a diverse portfolio of programs including <cite id="4">civil rights defense, media relations, and government affairs</cite>. Financially, the organization demonstrated significant growth in 2023, reporting <cite id="5">total revenue of $7,942,722</cite>, representing a <cite id="6">3-year revenue CAGR of 13.6%</cite>. The charity maintains an exceptional <cite id="7">overall score of 98.98.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, driven by perfect marks in culture and leadership. While its <cite id="8">program expense ratio is reported at 66.4%</cite> in recent filings, the organization also maintains a highly resilient financial position with <cite id="9">11.4 months of working capital</cite> in reserve. Beyond legal advocacy, CAIR facilitates <cite id="10">community empowerment and educational programs</cite> designed to counter Islamophobia and promote civic engagement across the United States.
